Items.Title: 宣布 GitHub + DEV 2023 黑客马拉松！ | Announcing the GitHub + DEV 2023 Hackathon!
Items.Description:
嘿，开发社区！

我希望你已经准备好迎接一些激动人心的消息。 宣布 GitHub + DEV 2023 黑客马拉松 🎉

从现在到 5 月 23 日，GitHub 提供了一个不容错过的机会，可以利用 GitHub Actions 或 GitHub Codespaces 使开源受益以换取奖品。 所有参与者都需要分享一篇 DEV 帖子，详细说明他们是如何创建项目的。 我们迫不及待地想看看你创造了什么！ 任何提交有效项目（包括在 DEV 上发布的官方提交帖子）的人都将自动进入，赢取各种精美奖品（包括高达 1,500 美元！）。

继续阅读以获取有关 GitHub Actions、GitHub Codespaces 的所有详细信息，以及如何加入黑客马拉松以获得赢得一些令人兴奋的奖品的机会。

什么是 GitHub 操作？

操作位于您存储代码、与同行协作、解决问题和发布开源软件的同一位置 — GitHub！

无论您是构建自定义操作还是将现有操作集成到黑客马拉松的工作流程中，我们都希望您在离开时能更好地理解 GitHub Actions 的价值——对您和开源社区而言。

我们很高兴看到您如何应对这一挑战！ 更多关于 GitHub Actions 的信息。 查看#actionshackathon 和#actionshackathon21 的标签页，了解有关 GitHub Actions 工作流创意的一些灵感。

什么是 GitHub 代码空间？

您可以通过将开发容器文件配置到存储库（通常称为配置即代码）来为 GitHub Codespaces 自定义项目，这会为项目的所有用户创建可重复的代码空间配置。

您可以从浏览器连接到代码空间，也可以使用 Visual Studio Code 或 IntelliJ 等 IDE 在本地连接。

更多关于 GitHub Codespaces 的信息。

项目类别

指南：开发人员可以使用 Codespaces 设置一个环境来对问题进行分类和审查拉取请求。 指南：使用 Codespaces 或 Actions 为 DevOps 流程构建自动化工作流程！ 这可能包括自动化测试、自定义部署脚本或持续集成/持续部署 (CI/CD) 管道。

  • 有趣的物联网：与物联网集成的酷项目。

指南：使用代码空间或操作来管理和监控物联网设备，包括管理配置、更新固件以及收集和分析数据等任务。

  • 电话友好：为移动设备构建的项目（PWA 就绪，iOS/Android）

指南：开发人员可以使用 Codespaces 或 Actions 创建可在 iOS 和 Android 设备上运行的移动应用程序，以及为其 PWA 就绪应用程序设置自动化工作流和 CI/CD 管道。

  • 指导：对于这个类别，我们正在寻找一些真正愚蠢和/或有趣的提交。 自由地梦想大而荒谬——并利用 GitHub 提供的任何功能。

    为什么要参与？

    开源软件背后的核心理念是代码、技术，当同行评审既征求又易于访问时，我们使用的服务会更强大、更安全。 要真正参与开源社区，您（当然）需要参与！ 如果这还不够刺激，我们还有一些甜蜜的奖品！

    五名大奖获得者（每个类别一名）：

    • 价值 1,500 美元的礼品卡或等价物
    • 价值 300 美元的 Forem 商店信用额度
    • 开发贴纸包
    • 亚军奖品（总计 10 个 - 所有类别）：

      • 价值 150 美元的 Forem 商店信用额度
      • 开发贴纸包
      • 参与者（具有有效项目）：

        • 开发贴纸包
        • 包括运费。 如果在美国境外运输，可能适用国际关税和关税。 请研究您当地的进口法律以获取有关海关费用的更多信息，或如有任何问题，请联系shop@forem.com 。

          如何参加 GitHub + DEV 2023 黑客马拉松

          使用以下语言之一：

          请务必解决模板中的每个提示和说明。

          附加说明和规则：

    • 我们鼓励您使用#GitHubHack23标签在 DEV 上分享更新帖子，让我们及时了解您的进展（提示：在所有 GitHub + DEV Hackathon 相关帖子的降价标题中使用series: [“series name”]以链接所有系列内容）
    • 允许多次提交
    • 如果您与任何人合作，请在您的提交帖子中列出他们的 DEV 句柄，以便我们可以为您的整个团队颁发个人资料徽章！ 谢谢你的理解！

    无需购买。 仅对 18 岁以上开放。 比赛报名期结束于{比赛结束日期}，UTC 时间晚上 11:59。 在法律或法规禁止或限制的情况下，比赛无效。 所有参赛作品必须是新项目并在黑客马拉松期间创建。 有关官方规则，请参阅比赛公告页面和一般比赛官方规则。

    社区支持

    要询问有关 GitHub 或本次比赛规则的任何问题，请在官方帮助线程中发表评论。 我们的团队将监控这个空间，与 GitHub 团队合作回答您的问题。

    需要一些外部激励和指导吗？ 谁不会！ 我们鼓励所有参与者参与我们的社区讨论线程，您可以在构建应用程序时分享您的想法并从 DEV 社区获得改进建议。 您还可以使用此线程分享您在获得他人支持的过程中取得的进步。

    重要日期🗓

    • 我们很高兴您能与我们在 GitHub 的朋友一起参加这个全新的黑客马拉松。 玩得开心，学到很多东西，并随时关注我们。

      祝你好运，编码愉快！ 🍀 🍀



原文:

Hey, DEV community!

I hope you're ready for some exciting news. GitHub is launching their latest hackathon here on DEV Community where you can build a new application in the open, learn something new, and maybe win some awesome prizes in the process!

Announcing the GitHub + DEV 2023 Hackathon 🎉

From now until May 23rd, GitHub is offering an unmissable opportunity to utilize GitHub Actions or GitHub Codespaces to benefit open source in exchange for prizes. All participants will need to share a DEV post detailing how they created their project. We can't wait to see what you create! Anyone who submits a valid project (including an official submission post, published on DEV) will be automatically entered to win a variety of fantastic prizes (including up to $1,500 USD!).

Keep reading to get all the details on GitHub Actions, GitHub Codespaces, and how you can join the hackathon for a chance to win some exciting prizes.

What Are GitHub Actions?

GitHub Actions are individual tasks that, when combined, result in a custom workflow that’s all your own!

GitHub Actions allow you to automate and customize your software development workflows. Actions are located in the same place you store code, collaborate with peers, solve issues, and ship open source software — GitHub!

Whether you build a custom action or integrate an existing one into your workflow for the hackathon, we hope that you’ll walk away with a greater understanding of the value of GitHub Actions — for you and the open source community.

We’re excited to see how you approach this challenge! More on GitHub Actions here. Check out the tag pages for the #actionshackathon and #actionshackathon21 for some inspiration for GitHub Actions workflow ideas.

What are GitHub Codespaces?

GitHub Codespaces are development environments hosted in the cloud. You can customize your project for GitHub Codespaces by configuring dev container files to your repository (often known as Configuration-as-Code), which creates a repeatable codespace configuration for all users of your project.

GitHub Codespaces run on a variety of VM-based compute options hosted by GitHub, which you can configure from 2 core machines up to 32 core machines. You can connect to your codespaces from the browser or locally using an IDE like Visual Studio Code or IntelliJ.

More on GitHub Codespaces here.

Project Categories

The GitHub + DEV 2023 Hackathon is calling for projects in the following five categories:

  • Maintainer Must-Haves: Make the lives of Open Source maintainers easier.

Guidance: Developers can use Codespaces to set up an environment to triage issues and review pull requests. Actions can also be used to build tools and resources to help developers create better documentation.

  • DIY Deployments: Improve the deployment process for open source projects.

Guidance: Build automation workflows for DevOps processes using Codespaces or Actions! This could include automated testing, custom deployment scripts, or continuous integration/continuous deployment (CI/CD) pipelines.

  • Interesting IoT: Cool projects that integrate with IoT.

Guidance: Use Codespaces or Actions to manage and monitor IoT devices, including tasks like managing configurations, updating firmware, and collecting and analyzing data.

  • Phone Friendly: Projects built for Mobile (PWA readiness, iOS/Android)

Guidance: Developers can use Codespaces or Actions to create mobile applications that work on both iOS and Android devices, as well as set up automation workflows and CI/CD pipelines for their PWA ready apps.

  • Wacky Wildcards: Build a random app that doesn’t fit into one of the categories above.

Guidance: With this category, we are looking for some truly silly and/or fun submissions. Feel free to dream big and ridiculously — and utilize any feature that GitHub offers.

Why Participate?

The core philosophy behind open source software is code, technology, and the services we use are stronger and more secure when peer-review is both solicited and easily accessible. To be truly involved in the open source community, you (of course) need to participate! GitHub Actions and Codespaces are the perfect way to contribute to open source while optimizing your workflows.

If that’s not enough incentive, we also have some sweet, sweet prizes!

💰 Prizes 💰

Five Grand Prize Winners (one per category):

  • $1,500 USD gift card or equivalent
  • $300 USD credit to the Forem Shop
  • DEV Sticker Pack
  • GitHub Sticker Pack
  • DEV “GitHub Hackathon 2023” Grand Prize profile badge

Runner-Up Prizes (10 Total – across all categories):

  • $250 USD gift card or equivalent
  • $150 USD credit to the Forem Shop
  • DEV Sticker Pack
  • GitHub Sticker Pack
  • DEV “GitHub Hackathon 2023” Runner-Up profile badge

Participants (with a valid project):

  • DEV Sticker Pack
  • DEV “GitHub Hackathon 2023” participant profile badge

Cost of shipping included. If shipping outside of the U.S., international customs and duties may apply. Please research your local import laws for more information on customs fees or reach out to shop@forem.com with any questions.

How to Participate in the GitHub + DEV 2023 Hackathon

1) Sign up for a free GitHub account by clicking here or sign into your existing account.

2) Create a new and original app during the contest period using GitHub Actions or GitHub Codespaces that falls under one of the categories listed above. Use one of the following languages:

3) Share your code publicly on GitHub using one of the following permissive licenses for your code: MIT, Apache, BSD-2, BSD-3, or Commons Clause.

4) IMPORTANT: Use this post template to officially submit your application for the hackathon. Be sure to address every prompt and instruction in the template.

5) Be sure to publish your submission on DEV between April 25th and May 23rd (@ 11:59 PM UTC), and provide your app’s URL, screenshot, description, and source code

⚠️ Heads-up that you'll only be able to view our submission template linked above if you're logged into DEV.

Additional Notes and Rules:

  • We encourage you to share update posts on DEV using the #GitHubHack23 tag to keep us posted on your progress (hint: use series: [“series name”] in the markdown heading of all your GitHub + DEV Hackathon-related posts to link all content in a series)
  • Multiple submissions are allowed
  • If you collaborate with anyone, please list their DEV handles in your submission post so we can award a profile badge to your entire team! DEV does not handle prize-splitting, so in the event your project is named a Grand Prize-winner or runner-up, you will need to split those amongst yourselves. Thank you for understanding!

NO PURCHASE NECESSARY. Open only to 18+. Contest entry period ends {date of contest closure}, 11:59 PM UTC. Contest is void where prohibited or restricted by law or regulation. All entries must be new projects and created during the hackathon period. For Official Rules, see Contest Announcement Page and General Contest Official Rules.

Community Support

To ask any questions about GitHub or about the rules of this contest, leave a comment in the official help thread. Our team will be monitoring this space to answer your questions in collaboration with the GitHub team.

Need some external motivation and guidance? Who doesn’t! We encourage all participants to swing by our community discussion thread where you can share your ideas and get suggestions on improvements from the DEV community as you build your app. You can also use this thread to share your progress along the way to get support from others.

Important Dates 🗓

  • April 25th, 2023: Hackathon Begins
  • May 23rd, 2023: Hackathon Submission Due at 11:59 PM UTC
  • May 24th, 2023: Submission Judging begins
  • June 8th, 2023: Winners Announced and Prize Instructions Sent

We’re so excited for you to join us for this brand new hackathon with our friends at GitHub. Have fun, learn lots, and keep us posted along the way.

Good luck and happy coding! 🍀

Items.Title: 使用 API 网关进行批量请求处理 | Batch request processing with API Gateway
Items.Description:
批请求处理是 Web 开发中用于提高 API 性能的强大技术。 它允许开发人员将多个 API 请求分组到一个 HTTP 请求/响应周期中。 换句话说，可以将来自客户端的单个 API 请求转换为对一组后端服务器的多个 API 请求，并将响应聚合为对客户端的单个响应。 它可以显着减少客户端和服务器之间的往返次数。

在本文中，我们将探讨如何在Apache APISIX中实现批处理请求，并查看一些有用的用例。

它类似于微服务架构中广泛应用的API组合模式。


批量请求处理与多个 API 调用。

为什么要使用批量请求处理？

当客户端向服务器发送多个 API 请求时，每个请求都需要一个单独的 HTTP 请求/响应周期。 这可能会导致延迟增加、性能降低和服务器负载增加。 通过将多个请求分组为单个批处理请求，可以减少 HTTP 请求/响应周期的数量，从而提高性能并减少延迟。

批处理请求处理在需要在单个事务中检索或更新多个记录的情况下特别有用，例如：

  • 从数据库中检索多条记录
  • 更新数据库中的多条记录
  • 执行多个 API 请求以完成任务

批量请求处理的真实示例

示例 1：

假设您有一个显示用户提要的社交媒体应用程序，其中包括他们朋友的帖子和他们关注的页面。 要填充此提要，您需要发出多个 API 请求来检索必要的数据，例如：

  • 检索用户朋友的列表和他们关注的页面。
  • 对于每个朋友或页面，检索他们最近的帖子。

在传统方法中，您将分别执行这些 API 请求中的每一个。 首先，您检索用户好友列表，在第二个请求中，您获取每个用户好友的最新帖子。 这可能会导致延迟增加，尤其是当用户有大量朋友并关注许多页面时。

示例 2：

另一个例子，你有一个移动应用程序，它显示一个产品列表供用户浏览。 要填充此列表，您需要发出多个 API 请求以从远程服务器检索产品数据，例如：

  • 检索产品 ID 列表。
  • 对于每个产品 ID，检索产品详细信息（名称、描述、图像等）

示例 3：

想象一下，您有一个用于会议管理的 Web 应用程序，其中系统中有多个演讲者，并且您希望在单个网页上显示演讲者的会议和相关主题。 后端会议 API服务有两个不同的端点/speaker/{speakerId}/sessions和/speaker/{speakerId}/ topics 来公开此信息。 要显示会话和主题都属于一个演讲者，您可以从前端应用程序发送两个请求，这不是一个理想的解决方案。 相反，您可以使用API 网关将所有这些请求分组到一个 HTTP 请求中，如下一节所述。

使用 Apache APISIX API 网关进行批处理请求

要在 APISIX 中实现批量请求处理，可以使用batch-requests
插入。 此插件允许您在单个HTTP POST请求负载中定义一组 API 请求。 每个请求都可以有自己的 HTTP 方法、URL 路径、标头集和负载。 请参阅下面的 curl 请求命令以获取示例 3（会议 API 请求）：

 curl -i http://{API_GATEWAY_HOST_ADDRESS}/speaker -X POST -d \ '{ "pipeline": [ { "method": "GET", "path": "/speaker/1/topics" }, { "method": "GET", "path": "/speaker/1/sessions" } ] }'

当 APISIX 收到批处理请求时，批处理请求插件将解析负载并并行执行批处理中的每个请求。 该插件还将聚合来自每个请求的响应，并在单个 HTTP 响应中将它们返回给客户端。 请参阅下一个演示部分，了解如何逐步实现这一目标。

批量请求插件演示

在使用批处理请求插件之前，您需要安装 Apache APISIX。

先决条件

  • 您还可以使用Postman等简单工具与 API 进行交互。

配置后端服务（上游）

您需要为要将请求路由到的会议 API 配置后端服务。 这可以通过Admin API在 Apache APISIX 中添加上游服务器来完成。

 curl http://127.0.0.1:9180/apisix/admin/upstreams/1 -X PUT -d ' { "name": "Conferences API upstream", "desc": "Register Conferences API as the upstream", "type": "roundrobin", "scheme": "https", "nodes": { "conferenceapi.azurewebsites.net:443": 1 } }'

为批处理 API 创建路由

我们需要创建一个新路由来拦截对/speaker请求，并公开一个公共虚拟端点以使用[public-api](https://apisix.apache.org/docs/apisix/plugins/public-api/)进行批处理插入。

 curl http://127.0.0.1:9180/apisix/admin/routes/a -X PUT -d ' { "uri": "/speaker", "plugins": { "public-api": { "uri": "/apisix/batch-requests" } } }'

为演讲者的主题和会话端点创建路由

接下来，我们为演讲者的主题和会话端点创建另一个路由（ /speaker/*/topics和/speaker/*/topics路径匹配），以便 API 网关从批量请求中提取的单个请求以检索演讲者的主题或会话转发到负责的会议 API 端点，我们引用了现有的上游服务。

 curl http://127.0.0.1:9180/apisix/admin/routes/b -X PUT -d ' { "methods": ["GET"], "uris": ["/speaker/*/topics","/speaker/*/sessions"], "plugins": { "proxy-rewrite":{ "host":"conferenceapi.azurewebsites.net" } }, "upstream_id":"1" }'

您可能会注意到我们正在使用另一个代理重写插件并为会议 API 隐式指定主机地址。 否则，API 网关可以进行 DNS 转换并通过其 IP 地址请求会议 API。

测试批量请求处理

下面是如何在 APISIX 中使用批处理请求插件的示例：

 curl -i http://127.0.0.1:9080/speaker -X POST -d \ '{ "pipeline": [ { "method": "GET", "path": "/speaker/1/topics" }, { "method": "GET", "path": "/speaker/1/sessions" } ] }'

在此示例中，路由是为/speaker端点定义的，它支持通过 batch-requests 插件进行批量请求处理。 该插件配置了一组两个请求，每个请求都通过 ID 检索带有主题和会话的演讲者记录。 如果运行此命令，您将从 API 网关返回合并响应：

 [ { "body":"{
 \"collection\": {
 \"version\": \"1.0\",
 \"links\": [],
 \"items\": [
 {
 \"href\": \"https://conferenceapi.azurewebsites.net/topic/8\",
 \"data\": [
 {
 \"name\": \"Title\",
 \"value\": \"Microsoft\"
 }
 ],
 \"links\": [
 {
 \"rel\": \"http://tavis.net/rels/sessions\",
 \"href\": \"https://conferenceapi.azurewebsites.net/topic/8/sessions\"
 }
 ]
 },
 {

 }
 ],

 }
 ]
 }
 ],

 }
 }
}", "status":200, "headers":{ "Expires":"-1", "Connection":"keep-alive", "Pragma":"no-cache", "Content-Length":"953", "Server":"APISIX/3.2.0", "Content-Type":"application/vnd.collection+json", "X-AspNet-Version":"4.0.30319", "Cache-Control":"no-cache", "X-Powered-By":"ASP.NET" }, "reason":"OK" }, { "body":"{

 {

 },

 },

 }
 ],

 },

 \"href\": \"https://conferenceapi.azurewebsites.net/session/206/topics\"
 }
 ]
 }
 ],

 }
 }
}", "status":200, "headers":{ "Expires":"-1", "Connection":"keep-alive", "Pragma":"no-cache", "Content-Length":"961", "Server":"APISIX/3.2.0", "Content-Type":"application/vnd.collection+json", "X-AspNet-Version":"4.0.30319", "Cache-Control":"no-cache", "X-Powered-By":"ASP.NET" }, "reason":"OK" } ]

批量请求的最大大小受 API 网关限制。 您可以查看 API 网关文档以了解当前限制和请求超时配置。

外卖

  • 使用 API 网关进行批处理请求是一种提高 API 性能的有用技术。
  • 下一步

    使用 API 网关，还可以在响应数据中向您的用户提供某种形式的自定义聚合。 您可以使用serverless-function插件来执行自定义代码并合并来自后端服务的响应，并以不同的结构将其返回给 API 使用者。

    推荐内容

    • 为什么 Apache APISIX 是最好的 API 网关？
    • 什么是 API 网关策略？

    关于作者

    访问我的博客： www.iambobur.com



原文:

Batch request processing is a powerful technique used in web development to improve the performance of APIs. It allows developers to group multiple API requests into a single HTTP request/response cycle. In other words, a single API request from a client can be turned into multiple API requests to a set of backend servers, and the responses are aggregated into a single response to the client. It can significantly reduce the number of round trips between the client and server.

In this article, we'll explore how to implement batch request processing in Apache APISIX and look at some use cases where it can be beneficial.

It is similar to the API Composition pattern which is widely applied in Microservices architecture.


Batch request processing vs multiple API calls.

Why use batch request processing?

When a client sends multiple API requests to a server, each request requires a separate HTTP request/response cycle. This can result in increased latency, reduced performance, and increased server load. By grouping multiple requests into a single batch request, the number of HTTP request/response cycles can be reduced, resulting in improved performance and reduced latency.

Batch request processing can be particularly useful in scenarios where you need to retrieve or update multiple records in a single transaction, such as:

  • Retrieving multiple records from a database
  • Updating multiple records in a database
  • Executing multiple API requests to complete a task

Real-world examples for batch request processing

Example 1:

Suppose you have a social media application that displays a user's feed, which includes posts from their friends and pages they follow. To populate this feed, you need to make multiple API requests to retrieve the necessary data, such as:

  • Retrieve a list of the user's friends and the pages they follow.
  • For each friend or page, retrieve their recent posts.

In a traditional approach, you would perform each of these API requests separately. First, you retrieve a list of users’ friends and in the second request, you get recent posts for each user’s friend. This can result in increased latency, especially when the user has a large number of friends and follows many pages.

Example 2:

Another example, you have a mobile application that displays a list of products for users to browse. To populate this list, you need to make multiple API requests to retrieve product data from a remote server, such as:

  • Retrieve a list of product IDs.
  • For each product ID, retrieve the product details (name, description, image, etc.)

Example 3:

Imagine that you have a web app for conference management where there are multiple speakers in the system and you want to display a speaker’s sessions and related topics on a single web page. A backend Conference API service has two different endpoints /speaker/{speakerId}/sessions and /speaker/{speakerId}/topics to expose this information. To show both sessions and topics belong to a single speaker, you can send two requests from the frontend app which is not an ideal solution. Instead, you can use an API Gateway to group all of these requests into a single HTTP request, as it is explained in the next section.

Batch request processing with Apache APISIX API Gateway

To implement batch request processing in APISIX, you can use the batch-requests
plugin. This plugin allows you to define a set of API requests in a single HTTP POST request payload. Each request can have its own HTTP method, URL path, set of headers, and payload. See a curl request command below for example 3 (Conference API requests):


curl -i http://{API_GATEWAY_HOST_ADDRESS}/speaker -X POST -d \
'{ "pipeline": [ { "method": "GET", "path": "/speaker/1/topics" }, { "method": "GET", "path": "/speaker/1/sessions" } ]
}'

When a batch request is received by APISIX, the batch-requests plugin will parse the payload and execute each request in the batch in parallel. The plugin will also aggregate the responses from each request and return them in a single HTTP response to the client. See the next demo section to learn how to achieve this step-by-step.

Batch requests plugin demo

Before you can use the batch-requests plugin, you'll need to install Apache APISIX.

Prerequisites

  • Docker is used to install the containerized etcd and APISIX.
  • curl is used to send requests to APISIX Admin API. You can also use easy tools such as Postman to interact with the API.

APISIX can be easily installed and started with the following quickstart script:

curl -sL <https://run.api7.ai/apisix/quickstart> | sh

Configure the backend service (upstream)

You will need to configure the backend service for Conference API that you want to route requests to. This can be done by adding an upstream server in the Apache APISIX through the Admin API.

curl http://127.0.0.1:9180/apisix/admin/upstreams/1 -X PUT -d '
{ "name": "Conferences API upstream", "desc": "Register Conferences API as the upstream", "type": "roundrobin", "scheme": "https", "nodes": { "conferenceapi.azurewebsites.net:443": 1 }
}'

Create a Route for batch processing API

We need to create a new route that intercepts requests to /speaker and exposes a public virtual endpoint for batch processing using [public-api](https://apisix.apache.org/docs/apisix/plugins/public-api/) plugin.

curl http://127.0.0.1:9180/apisix/admin/routes/a -X PUT -d '
{ "uri": "/speaker", "plugins": { "public-api": { "uri": "/apisix/batch-requests" } }
}'

Create a Route for the speaker’s topics and sessions endpoints

Next, we create another route for the speaker’s topics and sessions endpoints (/speaker/*/topics and /speaker/*/topics paths matching) so that individual requests extracted by the API Gateway from batch requests to retrieve the speaker’s topics or sessions are forwarded to the responsible Conference API endpoints and we referenced to the existing upstream service.

curl http://127.0.0.1:9180/apisix/admin/routes/b -X PUT -d '
{ "methods": ["GET"], "uris": ["/speaker/*/topics","/speaker/*/sessions"], "plugins": { "proxy-rewrite":{ "host":"conferenceapi.azurewebsites.net" } }, "upstream_id":"1"
}'

You may notice that we are using another proxy-rewrite plugin and specifying implicitly host address for Conference API. Otherwise, API Gateway can make DNS conversion and request the Conference API by its IP address.

Test batch request processing

Here's an example of how to use the batch-requests plugin in APISIX:

curl -i http://127.0.0.1:9080/speaker -X POST -d \
'{ "pipeline": [ { "method": "GET", "path": "/speaker/1/topics" }, { "method": "GET", "path": "/speaker/1/sessions" } ]
}'

In this example, the route is defined for the /speaker endpoint, which supports batch request processing via the batch-requests plugin. The plugin is configured with a set of two requests, each retrieving a speaker record by ID with topics and sessions. If you run this command, you will get a merged response back from the API Gateway:

[ { "body":"{
 "collection": {
 "version": "1.0",
 "links": [],
 "items": [
 {
 "href": "https://conferenceapi.azurewebsites.net/topic/8",

 }
 ],

 }

 },

 ],

]

The maximum size of a batch request is limited by API Gateway. You can check the API Gateway documentation for the current limits and request timeout configuration.

Takeaways

  • Batch request processing with API Gateway can be a useful technique for improving the performance of your API.
  • Apache APISIX provides a plugin called batch-requests that allows developers to implement batch request processing easily.

Next steps

With API Gateway, it is also possible to provide some form of custom aggregation in the response data to your users. You can use the serverless-function plugin to execute custom code and merge the response from backend services and return it to the API consumer in a different structure.

Items.Title: Dev.to的机器人入侵 | The robot invasion of Dev.to
Items.Description:
在之前的一篇文章中，我一直在抱怨 Dev.to 的质量太差，或者至少抱怨信噪比低得令人难以置信。

作者与读者

正如我所解释的，平台和读者的动机与（大多数）作者的动机不一致。

旁注：并非所有作者。 有些人似乎确实喜欢写作和分享知识。 但从发表的内容来看，这似乎是极少数。

现在，我个人认为作者通过在线分享他们的知识而获得个人利益是件好事。 我喜欢写作，但我也喜欢被阅读，如果未来的招聘人员喜欢我写的东西，那将是一个很好的动力助推器。

但是有两种方法可以做到这一点。 你要么花很长时间精心制作一篇有趣、有洞察力的文章……要么你随便发垃圾邮件。

自从我上一篇抱怨现状的文章（发表于 2021 年 7 月 6 日）以来，我们经历了一场人工智能革命。 而现在，我会说机器人入侵已经开始，但事实并非如此。 机器人已经入侵，他们赢了。 在“相关”部分搜索有趣的文章，GPT 生成了大量文章。 几乎所有这些都是通过 ChatGPT 进行的：您可以看出 ChatGPT 训练固有的微妙味道，例如它倾向于以 5 到 10 个要点进行回复。

我们确实生活在这样一个时代，你无法确定某些内容是人工智能生成的，还是只是人类编写的内容不佳。 值得庆幸的是，我们有诸如zeroGPT之类的工具，可以高精度地检测某些东西是否是由 AI 生成的。

我什至怀疑这个过程是自动化的。 在某个地方写一个包含 20 个提示的列表，然后编写一个脚本定期从 ChatGPT 直接复制粘贴到 Dev.to 是微不足道的。 好了，为您的个人品牌打造的 20 篇“有趣”文章。

平台上（几乎）没有人工智能辅助的文章

都是生成的。 有时很明显作者花了不到一分钟的时间修改了几个词并添加了标题甚至是介绍。 但文章完全是人工智能生成的。

很少有帖子实际上是“聊天辅助”的，这意味着 GPT 用于撰写文章的部分内容，但文章的大部分内容都是由人自己编写的。 其中大部分是完全、完全、从 ChatGPT 复制粘贴的。

我检查了 100 多篇人工智能生成的文章，只有一两篇被检测为“人工智能辅助”。 我不喜欢人工智能辅助写作，但这不是问题所在。 问题是 AI 生成和极度懒惰导致糟糕的内容。

显然，这是非常糟糕的。

我们的人工智能技术令人印象深刻。 革命性的，甚至。 但我们仍然没有突破 AGI 的里程碑，即人工智能实际上可以产生新的、有趣的、有洞察力的内容。

所以这只会放大 Dev.to 上最大的问题：缺乏好的、有趣的内容，以及这些内容淹没在平庸的海洋中。

这是我个人对 Dev.to 的AI 辅助内容指南的解读。 我提到这是对指南的个人解释，因为我个人觉得它们不清楚而且有点混乱。

在我检测到的 100 多个帖子中，AI 生成（不是 AI 辅助，而是 100% 生成），绝​​对没有一个披露该信息。

该指南还提到人工智能辅助和人工智能生成的文章不应该：

发布的主要目的是建立个人品牌、建立社交媒体影响力或获得影响力。

虽然这条规则在精神上是伟大的，但它绝对无法执行，因此毫无用处。 没有读心的能力，你就无法知道作者的主要意图。

可以做什么？

以下是一些表演建议：

  • 强化和澄清指南：我个人的观点是，AI 生成的内容，甚至可能是 AI 增强的内容，在 Dev.to 或任何追求低质量的出版网站上绝对没有立足之地。 任何违反此规则的行为都应被禁止。

  • 雇用额外的版主来执行这些规则。 尽管我喜欢报道违反准则的文章以提高 Dev.to 的整体质量，但这感觉不仅像从海洋中移走一滴水，而是将那滴水传递给可能或可能会的另一个人不决定它属于海洋。

我自己没有找到任何方式成为版主，只是一个“受信任的用户”，这似乎并没有赋予额外的版主权力。

  • 悬而未决的成果：在每个新出版物上对 GPT 生成的文本实施自动检测。 该技术已经存在并且已经可靠且强大。

结论

我喜欢在 Dev.to 上写作。 我喜欢在充满狂热开发者的平台上发布。 我喜欢社区意识，也喜欢团队为策划内容所做的努力。 我的最后两篇文章进入了“每周前 7 名”，我非常感谢团队选择了我。

但我不喜欢在 Dev.to 上阅读。 很难在机器人的海洋中找到有见地、有趣的内容，这些机器人都在大喊大叫以引起您的注意。

我确实希望它在未来变得更好，但它不会自己发生。 我们需要对机器人做点什么。



原文:

In a previous article, I've been complaining about the sheer lack of quality on Dev.to, or at the very least about the incredibly low signal-to-noise ratio.

Authors Vs Readers

As I was explaining, the incentives of the platform and readers are at odds with the incentives of (most of) the authors.

The readers on Dev.to want good quality content which can easily be found.

The authors on Dev.to want recognition, personal branding, and sometimes just good old link building (linking to their personal product/pages for SEO).

Sidenote: Not all authors. Some do seem to enjoy writing and sharing knowledge. But looking at what's published, this seems to be an extreme minority.

Now, I personally think it's great that authors get personal benefits for sharing their knowledge online. I love writing, but I also love being read, and if a future recruiter love what I wrote, that's a good motivation booster.

But there are two ways to go for that. You either spend a long time carefully crafting an interesting, insightful article... or you just spam whatever.

Since my last article complaining about the state of affairs (Published on the 6 of July, 2021), we lived through an AI revolution. And now, I would say that the robot invasion have begun, but that's not true. The robots have already invaded, and they won. Dev.to belongs to the Robots.

The ChatGPT problem on Dev.to

Searching for interesting articles on the "relevant" section, a tremendous amount are GPT-generated. Almost all of them via ChatGPT: you can recognize the subtle flavor inherent to ChatGPT's training, such as its tendency to reply in 5 to 10 bullet points.

We do live in an age where you can't be sure that something was AI-generated, or is just poorly-written human content. Thankfully, we have tools such as zeroGPT to detect with high precision if something is AI-generated.

I even suspect the process to be automatized. It would be trivial to write a list of 20 prompts somewhere, then write a script to regularly copy-paste directly from ChatGPT to Dev.to. There you go, 20 "interesting" articles for your personal branding.

There are (almost) no AI-assisted articles on the platform

It's all generated. Sometimes it is clear the author took less than a minute modifying a few words and adding titles or even an introduction. But the articles are completely AI-generated.

Very few posts are actually "chat-assisted", meaning GPT was used to write parts of the article, but a human wrote big pieces of the article themselves. Most of them are entirely, completely, copy-pasted from ChatGPT.

I checked more than 100 AI-generated articles, only 1 or 2 were detected as "AI-assisted". I'm not a fan of AI-assisting for writing, but this isn't the problem here. The problem is AI-generation and extreme laziness leading to terrible content.

Obviously, this is very bad.

Our AI technology is impressive. Revolutionary, even. But we still haven't breached the AGI milestone where an AI can actually produce new, interesting, insightful content.

So this is only amplifying the biggest issue on Dev.to: the lack of good, interesting content, and the drowning of such content in a sea of mediocrity.

What are Dev.to guidelines on this?

This is my personal interpretation of Dev.to's Guidelines for AI-assisted content..

I mention this is a personal interpretation of the guidelines, because I personally find them unclear and a tad confusing.

It seems that Dev.to authorizes AI-generated content, under the conditions that the post discloses that the content was AI-generated.

On the 100+ posts I detected were AI-generated (not AI-assisted, but 100% generated), absolutely none disclosed that information.

The guidelines also mention that AI-assisted and AI-generated articles should not:

Be published with the main purpose of building a personal brand, building a social media presence, or gaining clout.

While this rule is great in spirit, it is absolutely unenforceable and therefore useless. Without the ability to read minds, you cannot know the main intentions of an author.

What can be done?

Here are some acting suggestions:

  • Hardening & clarifying the guidelines: My personal opinion is that AI-generated content, and maybe even AI-enhanced content, have absolutely no place on Dev.to or any publishing website that strives for a modicum of quality. Any breaking of this rule should be cause for a ban.

  • Hiring extra moderators to enforce those rules. As much as I love to report guidelines-breaking articles in an effort to increase Dev.to's overall quality, it feels not only like removing a drop of water from the ocean, but instead passing that drop of water to another person who might or might not decide it belongs in the ocean.

I did not find any way to become a moderator myself, just a "trusted user", which doesn't seem to confer extra moderation power.

  • The lowest hanging fruit: Implementing auto-detection for GPT-generated text on each new publication. The technology exists and is already reliable and robust.

Conclusion

I love writing on Dev.to. I love publishing on a platform full of enthusiast developers. I love the sense of community, and I love the efforts from the team to curate content. My last two articles made it to 'Top 7 weekly', and I'm infinitely grateful to the team for choosing me.

But I don't love reading on Dev.to. It's just so hard to find insightful, interesting content in the sea of robots all yelling for your attention.

I do hope it gets better in the future, but it won't happen by itself. We need to do something about the robots.

开发人员您好，如果您想学习 Docker 和容器并从 DevOps 和开发人员的角度寻找最好的 Docker 课程，那么您来对地方了。 早些时候，我分享了免费的 DevOps 课程和Docker 课程，在本文中，我将分享最适合软件工程师和 DevOps 的 Docker 在线课程。

对于软件开发人员而言，Docker 是游戏规则的改变者，因为它彻底改变了您在物理设备和云上部署应用程序的方式。

使用 Docker 的一个关键优势是您可以以相同的方式部署任何类型的微服务。 它还使您可以更轻松地使用 Kubernetes 扩展您的服务。 你可以进一步阅读我之前的帖子《为什么每个开发者都应该学习Docker》，以更多地了解Docker对于现代软件和云计算世界的开发和发展的优势。

同时，如果您是初学者或以前从未使用过 Docker，学习 Docker 可能是一个挑战，但在适当的指导和正确的资源的帮助下，您可以轻松理解 Docker。 一旦了解了基本的 Docker 概念和命令，使用 Java 或任何其他编程语言就会变得更加容易。

顺便说一下，学习 Docker 和 Kubernetes 是独立于 Java、Python 或任何其他编程语言的。 任何程序员都可以使用 Docker 将他们的应用程序部署为容器，并使用 Kubernetes 来扩展他们的应用程序。

这就是为什么我还建议您首先从通用但全面的课程开始，如Docker 和 Kubernetes：实用指南 [2023 版] ，然后转到特定课程，您将在其中学习如何使用 Docker 部署 Python、Java 和 Spring boot 应用程序和库伯内特。

它还提供初学者和高级 Docker 课程，以满足初学者和有经验的 Java 开发人员的需求。 您还可以使用这些课程来准备 Docker 认证，例如 DCA（Docker 认证助理）和其他 Docker 考试。

这是 Udemy 最好的 Docker 在线课程之一。 本课程专为那些绝对是 docker 初学者的人设计。 讲师从初学者级别的知识开始，然后逐渐进入下一个级别的 docker 概念。

该课程涵盖所有重要概念，例如 docker 命令、docker run、docker 图像、docker-compose、docker 引擎、安全性和网络。

要求
本课程的讲师是 Udemy 最畅销的讲师之一。 已有超过六万七千名学生注册了这门课程。

这是加入本课程的链接 - Docker for the Absolute Beginner - Hands-On - DevOps

要求 1.基本的Linux和系统操作。 讲师不着急地详细解释了每个概念。 这是一个长达六个小时的课程。 所以如果你有兴趣深入学习这些概念，你应该去学习这门课程。

这个指导项目的主要目标是使用 docker 容器创建一个真实世界的应用程序。

本课程/项目适合初学者。 该课程分为几个任务。 每项任务都将指导您进一步使用 docker 容器创建真实世界的应用程序。

一、docker基础知识

Items.Title: 我在 YouTube 上被 ThePrimeagen 拷问了 | I Have Been Grilled by ThePrimeagen on YouTube
Items.Description:
我的文章Keep Calm and Just Say No To Coding Challenges收到了很多很棒的反馈，但没有一个比ThePrimeagen在他的 YouTube 频道上现场质询更有趣了。

三个不同的朋友提醒我这个视频，其中一个以这种方式向我介绍 ThePrimeagen：

正如您将在视频中看到的那样，他的风格有些人喜欢，有些人讨厌。

很公平。 但就我而言，我发现他的视频绝对令人愉快。

有人叫我试试拍视频，我拒绝了。 我想我会是一个非常蹩脚的 YouTuber。 我只是一个写东西的家伙。

但是 ThePrimeagen 在这方面非常出色。


看到我的文章在 YouTube 上实时呈现是一种很酷很有趣的体验。

经过如此巧妙的建立，我希望他不仅会同意我的看法。

确实：

好的，只是让你知道。 大家猜猜怎么着？ 这个人只是给了你最好的方法，让你在许多工作面试中被拒绝。 也许这个人并不那么需要工作。 因此可以这样说：“你是做什么的？哦，你就像 93% 的科技公司一样，你有编码挑战？大声笑。抱歉，我不接受他们。它在我脑海中留下了一个哈希托盘。所以不，谢谢。为了你的工作和面试过程”。

那开始很好。

唉，这多半是个误会……

到目前为止，在没有阅读任何其他内容的情况下，我目前的看法是这是我听过的最糟糕的建议。 实事求是：公司确实告诉您存在秘密握手。 它涉及了解基本数据结构以及如何应用它们。 所以你可以花时间去学习，好好学习，然后走进去，秘密握手，然后直接走进门。 或者你可以拒绝所有合理的工作。

这不合理吗？

我的回答会很无聊：我同意所有这些。

我们对我文章中编码挑战的含义有一个基本的误解。 我不是指算法面试。

混淆是我的错，我没有定义这个词。 并非所有挑战都是平等的，评论中对此进行了大量讨论。

发生的事情是，我在看到一位朋友在这种编码挑战上浪费了太多时间和士气后写了这篇文章：

我们是一家音乐播放器应用程序公司，因此我们挑战您做一个简化的音乐播放器应用程序，估计躺时间为四个小时，然后我们将根据未知标准评估您的代码质量。

什么是好的招聘标准？

在之前文章的评论中，我已经懒洋洋地描述了各种挑战的好坏。

等等，这是什么意思？

在文献中实际上有一个好的招聘标准的真正定义，我会这样总结。

一个好的招聘标准必须是中肯的、稳定的、现实的、非常明确的。

让我们看看为什么编码挑战（我想到的那些）很糟糕

  • 您的代码之美在于旁观者的眼中。
  • 团队内部的人，他们对构建音乐播放器了如指掌，并且设计了这个挑战，可以用 42 种不同的方式批评你的工作。 但事实是，在那里工作的人刚开始时并不知道他们今天所知道的一切。 我们开发人员是学习者，不要因为我们在给定时间不知道所有事情而评判我们。 发现我们的潜力。
  • 候选人过度设计编码挑战的原因之一是他们的要求非常模糊。 当然，他们描述了功能，但没有描述标准。 候选人不知道他们将根据什么标准进行评判。

因此，如果您的挑战符合这四个标准，并且 Netflix 的算法挑战可能符合要求，那么一切都很好。

但正如您所看到的，我想到的编码挑战显然是不行的，这是对别人坏主意的狂热崇拜。

浪费你的时间和公司的时间

当您对对您来说很重要的事情说不时，总是对其他事情说是。

  • 你保护你的时间。
  • 你保护你的士气。
  • 你保护你的自尊。

因为我是从候选人的角度写的，所以我没有提到的一件事是他们也在为公司浪费时间。 这与 Netflix 无关，因为我猜那里有很多有才华的候选人。

但是很多好的公司都有空缺3个月、6个月的职位。

一个明确的松散的方法。

你确实有权说不

我谈到了公司的观点，但我会警告你不要在面试中这样做。 我不建议你说是有原因的：

贵公司的招聘做法在我脑海中留下了一个哈希托盘。 所以不，谢谢。 对于您的工作和面试过程。

相反，我推荐这个：

我必须对你透明，我过去在编码挑战方面有过糟糕的经历，我决定不再接受它们。

这里的不同之处在于你不会评判他们并让他们处于守势。 您对自己的需求很自信。

那些会因为你陈述你的需求而生气的公司

  1. 是少数
  2. 子弹躲过

所以我完全支持我原来的论点。

您有权选择何时说不

最后我告诉你有权说不，我不是劝你一直说不。

如果你在你梦想的公司申请，不要说不，投入时间。

如果你一个月只有 2-3 家公司面试你，你的下一步是产生更多的线索，你可以拖延说不。

另一方面，如果你从不使用你说不的权利，那么这个权利就毫无用处。

我所做的是，当我被一些我真的不想与之合作的公司面试时，我开始说不。 一开始很尴尬，但我没有上面说的那种压力。 现在我完全适应它，而且我知道它通常有效。 而且你不用担心我找不到工作。

没有难受的感觉

现在 ThePrimeagen，如果你读过我的话，我真的会笑你的视频。

你甚至给了我制作第二集的希望。

招聘被打破。 哦，伙计，看看这个，有这么多好东西。 看看这个，我们这里有这么多碎片。

好吧，今天让我们阅读“保持冷静，对编码挑战说不”

那确实很棒。

我可以推荐我三年前的第一次咆哮，谁开始了这个系列赛。

一旦暂停，jmfayard 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，jmfayard 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，jmfayard 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 jmfayard 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 jmfayard：

降低 jmfayard 的所有帖子的可见度

报告其他不当行为

取消标记 jmfayard 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

I received way lots of cool feedback for my article Keep Calm and Just Say No To Coding Challenges but none was as fun as being live grilled by ThePrimeagen on his YouTube channel.

ThePrimeagen's answer to my article

I was alerted about this video by three different friends, one presenting me ThePrimeagen this way:

Primeagen is an engineer doing impressive stuff at Netflix, he is very active in the Rust community. As you will see in the video, he has a style that some people love and some people hate.

Fair enough. But for my part, I found his video absolutely delightful.

Some people told me to try out making videos, but I declined. I think I would be a pretty lame YouTuber. I'm just a dude who write words about stuff.

But ThePrimeagen is brilliant at it.


It was a cool funny experience to see my article come to life in real time on YouTube.

After such an artful build-up, I was hoping he would not just only agree with me.

And indeed:

Ok, just so you know. Hey guess what everybody? This person just gave you the best way to get rejected from many job interviews. Perhaps this person doesn't need a job that much. And therefore can go like this: "What do you do? Oh you are like 93% of tech companies and you have a coding challenge? LOL. Sorry, I don't accept them. It leaves an hash tray in my mind. So no, thank you. To both your job and your interview process".

That started well.

Alas that's mostly a misunderstanding...

So far, without reading anything else, my current take is that it's some of the worst advice I've ever heard. Real talk: you literally have been told by companies that there is a secret handshake. It involves knowing basic data structures, and how to apply them. So you can go and study for height hours, get good at that, walk in, doing the secret handshake and go right insight the door. Or you can say no to every reasonable job out there.

Is that unreasonable?

My answer will be boring: I agree with all of that.

We have a basic misunderstanding of what coding challenge meant in the context of my article. I didn't mean an algorithmic interview.

And the confusion is my fault, I didn't define the term. Not all challenges are created equals, and that was heavily discussed in the comments.

What happened is that I wrote the article after seeing a friend waste way too many time and morale with a coding challenge of that sort:

We are a music player app company, so we challenge you to do a simplified music player app, with a lying time estimate of four hours, and then we will evaluate the quality of your code according to unknown criteria.

What's a good hiring criteria?

In the comments of previous articles, I have lazily described all kind of challenges as good or bad.

Wait, what does that mean?

There is actually a real definition of a good hiring criteria in the literature, which I would summarise this way.

A good hiring criteria must be pertinent, stable, realistic and very clear.

Let see why coding challenges (the ones I had in mind) suck

  • ✅ Coding challenges are pertinent in the sense that the simplified music app has a clear link to the real music app the company is building.
  • ❌ Coding challenges are not stable, meaning if you present the same coding challenge to different companies, or even different people inside the companies, you will get "Look Good To Me", "Not Our Level Of Quality (TM)" and everything above and under that. The beauty of your code is in the eye of the beholder.
  • ❌ Coding challenges are not realistic for someone outside of the team (that's what the term means). People inside the team, who know everything about building a music player, and who designed this challenge, can criticise your work in 42 different ways. But truth here is that people working there didn't know everything they know today when they started. We developers are learners, don't judge us because we don't know everything at a given time. Find out our potential.
  • ❌ Coding challenges are not very clear. One of the reason candidates over-engineer shit with coding challenges is that they the requirements are super vague. Sure they describe the features, but not the criteria. The candidates can't know under which criteria they will be judged.

So if your challenges fill the four criteria, and algorithmic challenges at Netflix probably does it, then all fine.

But as you can see, the coding challenges I had in mind are a clear no go, a cargo cult from someone's else bad idea.

Wasting both your time and the company's time

When you say NO to something important to you always say YES to something else.

  • You protect your time.
  • You protect your morale.
  • You protect your self respect.

One thing I didn't mention because I was writing from the candidate point of view is that they also waste time for the company. That's here not relevant for Netflix, because I guess that shitloads of talented candidates apply there.

But many good companies have posts that stay vacant for 3 months, 6 months.

A clear loose loose approach.

You Indeed Have the Right to Say NO

I talked about the company's perspective, but I would warn you against doing that in the interview. There is a reason why I didn't propose you to say:

Your company's hiring practices leaves an hash tray in my mind. So no, thank you. To both your job and your interview process.

Instead I recommended this:

I have to be transparent with you, I had bad experiences in the past with coding challenges and I have decided to stop accepting them.

The difference here is that you not judging them and putting them on the defensive. You are being assertive about your needs.

The companies that will be mad at you for stating your needs

  1. are the minority
  2. bullet dodged

So I completely stand by my original thesis.

You Have the Right to Choose When To Say No

Finally I told you the have the right to say NO, I didn't advise you to say NO all the time.

If you apply at your dream company, don't say NO, invest the time.

If you have only 2-3 companies a month that interview you, your next step is to generate more leads, you can procrastinate on saying NO.

On the other hand, if you never use your right to say NO, then that right is pretty useless.

What I did is that I started saying NO when I was interviewed by companies I didn't really wanted to work with anyway. It was awkward at first, but I didn't have the kind of pressure described above. Now I completely feel comfortable with it, and I know that it usually works. And you don't have to worry for me finding a job.

No hard feeling

Now ThePrimeagen, if you read me, I genuinely laughed at your video.

And you even gave me the hope to make a second episode.

Hiring is broken. Oh man, look at this, there are so many good ones. Look at this, we have so many pieces here.

Allright, for today let's read "Keep Calm and Just Say No To Coding Challenges"

That would be awesome indeed.

I can recommend my first rant, three years ago already, who started this serie.

Sanjampreet Singh - Apr 17

Philip John Basile - Apr 26

Items.Title: 减少 Next.js 应用程序的 Docker 镜像大小 | Reduce Docker Image size for your Next.js App
Items.Description:
首先，我希望你知道什么是 Docker，但如果你不知道：

你可以花时间了解它

另一方面，NextJS 是一个灵活的 React 框架，它为您提供构建块来创建快速的 Web 应用程序。

另一方面，NextJS 是一个灵活的 React 框架，它为您提供构建块来创建快速的 Web 应用程序。

在我们优化任何东西之前，我们必须先将应用程序 dockerize。 假设我们的应用程序名称是my-space 。 从...开始：

创建Dockerfile ：

忽略dockerignore中不需要的文件：

 node_modules .next .vscode .gitignore README.md .dockerignore .git

这是关于如何对您的应用程序进行 docker 化的最基本示例，现在让我们构建它：

 docker build -t my-space .

现在看看尺寸：

难以置信，我们不能发布这张图片，它太重了！

缩小图像尺寸

使用高山

原始版本大小约为 1gb。

现在我们将转到高山版本：

现在大小缩小到1.65gb，​​小了 800mb 。 这是一个好的开始！

多阶段构建

多阶段构建对于那些努力优化 Dockerfiles 同时保持它们易于阅读和维护的人很有用。

我们将在Dockerfile中创建 2 个阶段。 我会称之为builder和runner

通过这种方式，我们可以删除图像中不需要的文件：

我们将从builder中选择文件并将其移动到我们最终将使用的runner ：

删除重复图层

你可以看到有东西在复制。 是的，我们为每个阶段安装两次依赖项。 尽管这有效并且项目大小仍然相同。 由于缓存和图层，图像大小仍然很大。

所以我们可以从构建阶段选择node_modules ：

现在的大小对于 NextJS 应用程序来说相当不错，低于 500mb

但我们仍然可以让它更轻！

输出文件跟踪

在构建期间， Next.js将自动跟踪每个页面及其依赖项，以确定部署应用程序生产版本所需的所有文件。

此功能有助于大大减少部署的大小。 以前，在使用 Docker 进行部署时，您需要安装包依赖项中的所有文件才能运行next start 。 从 Next.js 12 开始，您可以利用.next/目录中的输出文件跟踪来仅包含必要的文件。

在您的next.config.js文件中，启用独立输出

experimental: { outputStandalone: true, },

这将在.next/standalone中创建一个文件夹，然后可以在不安装node_modules情况下自行部署该文件夹。

对于大多数情况来说足够小
结论

这只是一个关于如何优化 docker 镜像大小的简单示例，您可以更深入地查看 Docker 文档以找到最适合您的应用程序的处理方法！



原文:

First thing first, I expect you to know what is Docker, but if you don’t:

Docker is an open platform for developing, shipping, and running applications

You can spend time learning about it

NextJS, in other hand, is a flexible React framework that gives you building blocks to create fast web applications.

NextJS, in other hand, is a flexible React framework that gives you building blocks to create fast web applications.

Dockerize your App

Before we optimize anything, we have to dockerize the application first. Let’s say our application name is my-space . Starting with:

Create the Dockerfile :

Ignore unnecessary files in dockerignore:

node_modules
.next
.vscode
.gitignore
README.md
.dockerignore
.git

Dockerize it:

This is the most basic example on how to dockerize your app, now let’s build it with:

docker build -t my-space .

Now look at the size:

*That’s just crazy, 2.42gb!!
*

Unbelievable right, we can’t publish this image, it’s just too heavy !

Reduce the image size

Use alpine

The Node.js Docker team maintains a node:alpine image tag and variants of it to match specific versions of the Alpine Linux distributions with those of the Node.js runtime. The original version size is about 1gb.

Now we will move to the alpine version:

Now the size shrank to 1.65gb, 800mb smaller. That’s a good start !

Multi-stages builds

Multi-stage builds are useful to anyone who has struggled to optimize Dockerfiles while keeping them easy to read and maintain.

We will create 2 stages in the Dockerfile . I will call it builder and runner

In this way we can get rid of unnecessary files in our image:

We will pick files from the builder and move it to the runner that we will eventually use:

*The size comes down to 1.36gb, about 300mb smaller, we are doing good !
*

Remove duplicate layers

You can see something is duplicating. Yes, we install the dependencies twice for each stage. Although this works and the project size is still the same. The image size is still big because of caching and layers.

So we can pick the node_modules from the build stage:

The size now is quite decent for a NextJS app, below 500mb

But we can still make it lighter !

Output File Tracing

During a build, Next.js will automatically trace each page and its dependencies to determine all of the files that are needed for deploying a production version of your application.

This feature helps reduce the size of deployments drastically. Previously, when deploying with Docker you would need to have all files from your package’s dependencies installed to run next start. Starting with Next.js 12, you can leverage Output File Tracing in the .next/ directory to only include the necessary files.

In your next.config.js file, enable the standalone output

experimental: {
    outputStandalone: true,
  },

This will create a folder at .next/standalone which can then be deployed on its own without installing node_modules.

*The size is now 176mb! Small enough for most cases
*

Conclusion

This is just a simple example on how to optimize your docker image sizes, you can look deeper in Docker docs to find the most suitable treatment for your app!

Items.Title: 如何建立黑客马拉松🚀 | How to build a Hackathon 🚀
Items.Description:
我们刚刚发布了新的 Hackathon NovuConnect 🤩 我们确保它非常容易参与，今天我将讨论如何参与。

以下是我要接触的东西：

  • 作为 Hackathon 的所有者，您可以获得什么
  • 评审项目
  • 确保贡献很容易。

不知道的朋友，我们还安排了HackSquad ，这是Hacktoberfast期间最大的比赛之一，一个月就有5000人参加😲

什么是黑客马拉松

黑客马拉松在互联网时代之前就存在了。 开发人员会把他们的大型计算机放在一起编程，然后互相竞争几个小时。
随着互联网的发展，开发人员开始同步做同样的事情。

你为什么要举办黑客马拉松？

通常，您会期待一些事情：

  • 品牌- 通过举办黑客马拉松，您可以让更多的程序员接触到您的工具。
  • 演示- 通常，每个 Hackathon 都会关注一个工具或一家公司； 让开发人员使用这些工具并提交项目将产生您以后可以使用的演示。
  • 招聘——总是有杰出的开发人员，他们可以在创新和代码质量方面让你疯狂； 这通常是找到优秀人才的最简单方法。
  • 创新——Hackathon可以将你的产品打开成为你从未想过的新事物； 这就是大公司通常资助大型黑客马拉松的原因。

在线黑客马拉松的问题

我有机会与不同的创始人交谈，了解什么是好的，什么是错的。 与此同时，大多数黑客马拉松都获得了极高的注册量。 因此，只有少数开发人员会真正参与或提交完整的项目。 在我看来，有几个错误：

  • 没有焦点——虽然开发人员想参与，但他们不知道要构建什么，因此为开发人员提供他们可以使用的主题将极大地增加他们的注意力。
  • 没有明确的截止日期——我不是在谈论最终的项目截止日期，而是关于项目的提醒。 最好让开发人员知道他们的时间范围并每隔几天提醒他们提交日期。
  • 缺乏资源或文档——让开发人员以任何语言做出贡献是件好事，但为他们提供工具会让事情变得更容易。 这就是为什么在 Novu，我们为多种类型的语言创建了一个SDK 。 我们还制作了优秀的文档，开发人员可以查看。
  • 缺乏规则和一般资源——没有好的规则，你会被问题淹没； 确保一切都清晰易懂。

奖品

奖品是个棘手的事情！
我可以说的一件事是永远不要运送马克杯🤗

我通常更喜欢数字价格； 在我们生活在开源中时，提供 GitHub 资助是很常见的。 但是，这里还有一些您应该考虑的奖品。

  • 订阅不同的网站，例如 Pluralsight 和 Udemy。
  • 给你的贡献者排名，让他们成为你的大使，并给他们特殊的徽章。
  • 雇用开发人员（极端）

评审项目

你通常应该在规则中加入你要测试的内容； 在我们的例子中，它是：

  • 创新与创造力
  • 有用性和实用性
  • 解决方案的质量和完整性
  • 用户体验与设计

开发人员需要知道他们被评判的是什么，这样他们才能将注意力放在正确的领域。

您应该考虑的另一个重要考虑因素是：我可以判断使用 COBOL 制作的项目吗？ 好吧，今天没有人使用 COBOL，但你明白我的意思。

如果您让开发人员以任何语言提交项目，您可能需要聘请一些专家（或从社区中聘请一些专家）来评判项目。

如何开始？

  • 建立一个开发者可以注册的登录页面 - 确保为他们注册时事通讯，因为这将有助于向他们发送有关活动的更新。
  • 邀请开发者加入你的 Discord——有很多，但如果你没有足够的支持，那将很难，因为它是“实时支持”。
  • 确保为开发人员提供他们可以选择的主题，因为这将使您和他们的生活更轻松。
  • 使用开发人员可以克隆的 README.md 文件创建一个新的存储库； 在文件中，添加项目标题、描述、屏幕截图以及有助于您（判断项目）和开发人员更加专注的每个细节。
  • 每隔几天向开发人员发送提醒； 如果可以的话，制作一些很酷的“代码”活动，您可以在其中与他们一起编写代码，向他们展示如何使用您的工具。
  • 玩得开心 - 这始终是必须的。

您是否注册了我们的ConnectNovu 黑客马拉松？ 超级好玩； 你有机会展示你的技能并玩一些通知😉

一旦暂停，novu 将无法发表评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

解除暂停后，novu 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，novu 的所有帖子都将隐藏起来，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 novu 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

笔记：

感谢您维护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以通过以下方式标记 novu：

使 novu 的所有帖子不那么显眼

报告其他不当行为

Unflagging novu will restore default visibility to their posts. Unflagning novu 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

We have just released our new Hackathon NovuConnect 🤩 We have made sure it's super easy to participate, and today I will talk about how.

Here are the things I am going to touch:

  • What can you get as an owner of the Hackathon
  • Judging projects
  • Making sure it's easy to contribute.

For those of you who don't know, we have also arranged HackSquad, one of the biggest competitions during Hacktoberfast, and we got 5000 participants in one month 😲

What is a Hackathon

Hackathons existed before the days of the internet; developers would bring their big computers, program together, and compete with each other for hours.
As the internet evolved, developers started to do the same thing synchronically.

Why would you want to make a Hackathon?

Usually, you would be expecting a few things:

  • Branding - By conducting a Hackathon, you can expose more programmers to your tools.
  • Demos - Usually, every Hackathon would focus on a tool or a company; having developers working with the tools and submitting projects will result in Demos you can later use.
  • Hiring - The are always exceptional developers that can get you something crazy both in innovation and quality of code; that's usually the easiest way to find great talents.
  • Innovation - Hackathon can open your product into new things you have never thought about; that's why big companies usually fund big Hackathons.

The problems with online Hackathons

I have had the opportunity to talk to different founders and understand what went well and wrong. At the same time, most hackathons got an extreme amount of registration. As a result, only a few developers would actually participate or submit a complete project. In my opinion, there are a few faults:

  • No Focus - while developers want to participate, they don't know what to build, so providing developers with topics they can use will tremendously increase their focus.
  • No clear deadline - I am not talking about the final project deadline but reminders about the project. It's good to let developers know their time frame and remind them about the submission date every few days.
  • Lack of resources or documentation - letting developers contribute in any language is nice, but providing them with tools would make it easier. That's why in Novu, we have created an SDK for many types of languages. We have also made an excellent documentation developers can look at.
  • Lack of rules and general resources - Without good rules, you will be spammed with questions; ensure everything is clear and understandable.

Prizes

Prizes are a tricky thing!
One thing I can say is never ship mugs 🤣

I usually prefer a digital price; it's very common to offer GitHub funding as we live in open-source. However, here are a few more prizes you should consider.

  • Subscriptions to different websites such as Pluralsight and Udemy.
  • Giving ranks to your contributors, making them your ambassadors, and giving them special badges.
  • Hire developers (extreme)

Judging Projects

You should usually put in the rules what you are going to test; in our case, it's:

  • Innovation and creativity
  • Usefulness and practicality
  • Quality and completeness of the solution
  • User experience and design

Developers need to know what they are being judged for so they can put their focus on the right areas.

Another significant consideration you should think is: Can I judge a project made with COBOL? Ok, nobody uses COBOL today, but you get what I mean.

If you let developers submit projects in any language, you might need to hire some experts (or take some from the community) to judge the projects.

How to start?

  • Build a landing page where developers can register - make sure to register them for the newsletter, as it will be helpful to send them updates about the event.
  • Invite developers to your Discord - there is a big, but if you don't have enough support, that would be hard as it's "live support."
  • Make sure you provide developers with topics they can choose as it will make your life and their life easier.
  • Make a new repository with a README.md file that developers can clone; in the file, add the project title, description, screenshot, and every detail that would help you (to judge the project) and the developer to have more focus.
  • Send reminders to developers every few days; if you can, make some cool "code" events where you can code with them to show them how to use your tool.
  • Have fun - it's always a must.

Have you registered for our ConnectNovu Hackathon? It's super fun; you have a chance to showcase your skills and play with some notifications 😉

迪马·苏哈列夫

发表于 4 月 28 日

它会让你大吃一惊

这是 GPT-4 扮演地下城主的角色，有我和我的 3 个朋友参加

我会把这个东西编码成一个网络游戏，这样你就不用为团队联系电话而烦恼了；）

转发推文让更多人知道它并关注我成为第一个知道游戏何时公开的人

你的四位英雄——Max Voltage、Cassie Chroma、Rex Rocket 和 Maxine Flash——发现自己身处霓虹城的中心，这是一个充满全息广告牌、太阳能摩天大楼和充满活力的夜生活的繁华大都市。 当您站在市中心时，全息新闻广播引起了您的注意。 广播显示，一个被称为 Pixel Phantom 的神秘恶棍一直在整个 Retrotopia 制造混乱，破坏数字基础设施并使曾经和平的 AI 生物变得敌对。

你的任务是追踪 Pixel Phantom 并结束他们的恐怖统治。 要开始您的旅程，您有以下三种选择：

  1. 调查附近拱廊最近发生的 AI 攻击（DC：10）
  2. 访问当地发明家的工作室以收集有关 Pixel Phantom（DC：8）的信息
  3. 在城市的数字地下网络中寻找线索（DC：12）

你的派对会做什么？

导电服——一种由高级塑料纤维制成的套装，可增强基于电的能力。 静电靴——产生电荷以增强运动和跳跃的靴子。 等离子手榴弹 — 充满挥发性等离子能量的球形塑料手榴弹。 能量电容器 — 用于为各种电子设备充电的便携式电池。 当您进入时，您会注意到曾经和平的 AI 生物变得充满敌意，攻击街机和顾客。 一行人决定在抵御侵略性 AI 生物的同时向当地人询问 Pixel Phantom。

动作：

  1. 选择一项行动或提出您自己的建议。

    脉冲手枪——一种发射集中能量脉冲的紧凑型能量武器。 紧急降落伞——用于紧急着陆的紧凑型塑料降落伞。 她向他们询问 Pixel Phantom 的情况，同时留意她的队友，因为他们正在抵挡 AI 生物。

    掷 d20 以查看 Cassie 是否可以从当地人那里收集有用的信息（DC：10）。

    他们还提到 Phantom 似乎正在使用 VR 街机作为破坏数字基础设施和控制 AI 生物的基地。

    有了这些新信息，您的团队就有了三个选择：

    1. 直奔废弃的 VR 街机去面对 Pixel Phantom（DC：12）
    2. 访问当地发明家的工作室以收集更多信息并可能升级您的装备（DC：8）
    3. 探索城市的数字地下网络，找到削弱 Pixel Phantom 对 AI 生物控制的方法（DC：10）

    你的派对会做什么？

    当您接近建筑物时，您会注意到曾经充满活力的霓虹灯已被险恶的像素化光芒所取代。 拱廊的入口由一群堕落的 AI 生物守卫。

    动作：

    1. 选择一项行动或提出您自己的建议。

      投掷 d20 以查看脾气暴躁的猫全息图是否成功分散了 AI 生物的注意力（DC：12）。

      他们注意到全息图，但很快意识到这不是威胁。 他们将注意力转回你的队伍，现在意识到你的存在，并准备攻击。

      当 AI 生物发动第一次攻击时，你的队伍每人失去 3 HP。

      动作：

      1. 选择一项行动或提出您自己的建议。

        为每个英雄投掷 d20 以确定他们的攻击是否成功：

        1. 为每个英雄掷骰子并提供结果。

          您只提供了两个卷（7 和 14）。 为剩下的英雄再掷两次 d20。

          为 Rex Rocket 和 Maxine Flash 掷 d6 以确定对 AI 生物造成的伤害。

          其中两个 AI 生物受到严重损坏，但其他生物没有受到伤害。

          为每位英雄投掷 d20，以确定他们是否可以躲避即将到来的攻击（DC：每位英雄 10）。

          最大电压（掷骰：12）- 成功躲避即将到来的攻击。

        2. 你的队伍还有机会攻击守卫入口的 AI 生物：

          1. 为每个英雄的攻击投掷 d20。

            投掷 d20 以查看 Maxine 是否可以成功控制转换（DC：12）。

            动作：

            1. 为每个英雄的攻击投掷 d20。

              最大电压（掷骰：13）- 成功使用他的电弧干扰器使 AI 生物瘫痪。

            2. 为最大电压掷 d6 以确定对 AI 生物造成的伤害。

              两个 AI 生物仍然存在，仍然对复古游戏般的环境迷失方向。

              动作：

              1. 为每个英雄的攻击投掷 d20。

                派对：

                1. 最大电压：17/20 HP
                2. 雷克斯火箭：17/20 HP
                3. 玛克辛闪电侠：14/20 HP
                4. 卡西色度：17/20 HP

                人工智能生物：

                1. 人工智能生物 3：10/10 HP（未损坏）

                剩下两只 AI 生物，一只受损，一只未受损。

                最大电压（掷骰：12）- 成功使用他的电弧干扰器禁用另一个 AI 生物。

              2. 为 Max Voltage 和 Rex Rocket 掷 d6 以确定对 AI 生物造成的伤害。

                入口现已清空，您的队伍可以进入废弃的 VR 拱廊，与 Pixel Phantom 对峙。

                当你小心翼翼地进入拱廊时，你会注意到房间中央有一个巨大的像素化门户，闪烁着诡异的光芒。 它似乎是影响 AI 生物的腐败的来源。

                动作：

                1. 选择一项行动或提出您自己的建议。

GPT-4: Welcome to Retrotopia, a realm where retro-futuristic aesthetics and innovative technologies collide. Your party of four heroes—Max Voltage, Cassie Chroma, Rex Rocket, and Maxine Flash—find themselves in the heart of Neon City, a bustling metropolis filled with holographic billboards, solar-powered skyscrapers, and vibrant nightlife. As you stand in the city center, a holographic news broadcast catches your attention. The broadcast reveals that a mysterious villain known as the Pixel Phantom has been causing chaos throughout Retrotopia, corrupting the digital infrastructure and turning once-peaceful AI creatures hostile.

Your quest is to track down the Pixel Phantom and put an end to their reign of terror. To begin your journey, you have three options:

  1. Investigate the recent AI attacks at a nearby arcade (DC: 10)
  2. Visit the local inventor's workshop to gather information on the Pixel Phantom (DC: 8)
  3. Search for clues in the city's digital underground network (DC: 12)

GPT-4: Max Voltage, your inventory consists of the following items:

  1. Arc Disruptor — A wrist-mounted energy weapon that emits powerful electrical discharges. DC: 12 to check aim accurately.
  2. Conductive Suit — A suit made of advanced plastic fibers that enhances electric-based abilities. DC: 10 to check amplify electric effects.
  3. Static Boots — Boots that generate electric charges for enhanced movement and jumps. DC: 11 to check avoid electric overload.
  4. Plasma Grenades — Spherical plastic grenades filled with volatile plasma energy. DC: 9 to check throw precisely.
  5. Energy Capacitor — A portable power cell used to charge various electric devices. DC: 8 to check connect/disconnect safely.

◇ What do you do? │ Maxine — we go to the arcade to ask locals about Pixel Phantom │

◇

GPT-4: Maxine leads the party to the nearby arcade, where the vibrant lights and sounds of retro games fill the air. As you enter, you notice that the once-peaceful AI creatures have become hostile, attacking the arcade machines and patrons. The party decides to ask the locals about the Pixel Phantom while fending off the aggressive AI creatures.

Actions:

  1. Max Voltage uses his Arc Disruptor to protect the party from the hostile AI creatures (DC: 12)
  2. Cassie Chroma hacks into the arcade's security system to help contain the AI creatures (DC: 10)
  3. Rex Rocket uses his Rocket Blasters to quickly navigate the arcade and gather information from the locals (DC: 12)

Choose an action or suggest your own.

◇ What do you do? │ rex — what do i have in inventory? │

◇

GPT-4: Rex Rocket, your inventory consists of the following items:

  1. Rocket Blasters — A set of powerful jet thrusters for high-speed aerial maneuvers. DC: 12 to check perform complex aerial stunts.

They also mention that the Phantom seems to be using the VR arcade as a base to corrupt the digital infrastructure and control the AI creatures.

With this new information, your party has three options:

  1. Head straight to the abandoned VR arcade to confront the Pixel Phantom (DC: 12)
  2. Visit the local inventor's workshop to gather more information and possibly upgrade your gear (DC: 8)
  3. Explore the city's digital underground network to find a way to weaken the Pixel Phantom's control over the AI creatures (DC: 10)

What will your party do?

◇ What do you do? │ Rex — we go to the arcade │

◇

GPT-4: Rex Rocket leads the party to the abandoned VR arcade on the outskirts of Neon City. As you approach the building, you notice that the once-vibrant neon lights have been replaced by a sinister, pixelated glow. The entrance to the arcade is guarded by a group of corrupted AI creatures.

Actions:

Roll a d20 to see if the grumpy cat hologram successfully distracts the AI creatures (DC: 12).

  2. Rex Rocket (Roll: 14) - Successfully dodges the incoming attack.
  3. Maxine Flash (Roll: 8) - Fails to dodge the attack and loses 3 HP.

  3. Maxine Flash uses her Vaporwave Wand (DC: 11)

  4. Cassette Grimoire — A spellbook on a series of cassettes containing ancient magic. DC: 10 to check decipher spells.
  5. Pixel Potion — A potion that temporarily grants the user pixelated invisibility. DC: 8 to check predict duration.

◇ What do you do? │ Maxine — i convert surroundings to retro game │

◇

Roll a d20 to see if Maxine can successfully control the transformation (DC: 12).

◇ What do you do? │ 13 │

◇

GPT-4: Maxine Flash successfully transforms the surroundings into a retro game-like environment. The AI creatures become momentarily disoriented, giving your party an advantage in combat.

Actions:

  1. Max Voltage uses his Arc Disruptor to disable the AI creatures (DC: 10, reduced due to the environment)

Items.Title: 学习 C# 中的依赖倒置原则（+ 示例） | Learn Dependency Inversion Principle in C# (+ Examples)

依赖倒置原则 (DIP)是SOLID 设计原则的基本原则，对于在C#中构建可维护和可扩展的软件系统至关重要。

通过遵循 DIP，开发人员可以将其应用程序的高级组件与低级实现细节分离，从而使代码更易于修改和测试。

本文旨在解释 C# 中的依赖倒置原则，并提供实用示例和指南，以帮助开发人员创建健壮且灵活的软件系统。

理解依赖倒置原则

依赖倒置原则 (DIP)是SOLID的五个原则之一，是面向对象编程和设计的基本构建块。 依赖倒置原则通过引入抽象来促进高层模块与低层模块的解耦。

相反，它们都应该依赖于抽象。

依赖倒置原则的好处

依赖倒置原则提供了几个好处，例如：

  1. 解耦：通过坚持DIP，代码变得更加模块化，更容易维护和扩展。
  2. 可测试性：解耦代码更容易测试，因为每个模块都可以独立测试。
  3. 可重用性：模块变得更可重用，因为它们较少依赖于其他模块。

要在 C# 中实现依赖倒置原则，您应该专注于创建抽象（接口或抽象类）并使高层和低层模块都依赖于它们。 通过这样做，您可以避免高层和低层模块之间的直接依赖。

依赖倒置原则 C# 示例

为了更好地理解 C# 中的依赖倒置原则，让我们来看一个示例。

没有依赖倒置原则

假设您有一个向用户发送通知的电子邮件通知系统。 这种设计有一个问题： EmailNotification类与SmtpClient类紧密耦合。 如果您决定更改电子邮件发送机制，则需要修改EmailNotification类。

实施依赖倒置原则

要实施依赖倒置原则，您将引入EmailNotification类和SmtpClient类都将依赖的抽象。 这个抽象将是一个名为IEmailSender接口。

 public interface IEmailSender { void Send(string emailAddress, string message); }

用依赖倒置原则重构示例

现在，让我们重构EmailNotification和SmtpClient类以依赖于IEmailSender接口。 首先，使SmtpClient类实现IEmailSender接口。

 public class SmtpClient : IEmailSender { public void Send(string emailAddress, string message) { // Implementation of email sending using SMTP } }

接下来，修改EmailNotification类以依赖于IEmailSender接口而不是SmtpClient类。 您将通过将IEmailSender的实例传递给EmailNotification类的构造函数来完成此操作。

 public class EmailNotification { private IEmailSender _emailSender; public EmailNotification(IEmailSender emailSender) { _emailSender = emailSender; } public void Send(string emailAddress, string message) { _emailSender.Send(emailAddress, message); } }

现在， EmailNotification类不再直接依赖于SmtpClient类。 如果需要更改电子邮件发送机制，可以在不修改EmailNotification类的情况下创建一个实现IEmailSender接口的新类。

依赖倒置原则和接口

接口在依赖倒置原则中的作用

接口在实现依赖倒置原则中起着至关重要的作用。 它们充当高级和低级模块所依赖的抽象，允许轻松解耦和灵活性。

在 C 中使用接口实现依赖倒置原则

在 C# 中，您可以利用接口有效地实现依赖倒置原则。 通过为您的抽象定义接口，您可以创建更易于维护、测试和扩展的松耦合代码。

依赖倒置原则与依赖注入

了解依赖注入

依赖注入 (DI)是一种通过提供来自外部源的依赖关系来解耦类的技术。 它经常与依赖倒置原则结合使用，以实现更好的解耦和可测试性。

在 C# 中实现依赖注入的常用技术有以下三种：

  1. 构造函数注入：通过类的构造函数注入依赖项。
  2. 属性注入：通过类的公共属性注入依赖项。
  3. 方法注入：注入依赖项作为方法的参数。

结合依赖倒置原则和依赖注入

通过结合依赖倒置原则和依赖注入，您可以创建高度解耦和可测试的代码。 依赖倒置原则确保模块依赖于抽象，而依赖注入提供了一种在运行时向模块提供这些抽象的方法。

结论

依赖倒置原则是面向对象编程中的一个强大概念，可以极大地提高 C# 代码的质量。 通过遵循这一原则，您可以创建更易于维护、可测试和可重用的代码。 通过将 DIP 与依赖注入相结合，您可以进一步增强应用程序的灵活性和可测试性。



原文:

The Dependency Inversion Principle (DIP) is a fundamental principle of the SOLID design principles that is crucial for building maintainable and extensible software systems in C#.

By adhering to the DIP, developers can decouple the high-level components of their applications from the low-level implementation details, resulting in code that is easier to modify and test.

This article aims to explain the Dependency Inversion Principle in C# and offer practical examples and guidelines to help developers create robust and flexible software systems.

Understanding the Dependency Inversion Principle

Dependency Inversion Principle (DIP) is one of the five principles of SOLID, which are the essential building blocks of object-oriented programming and design. The Dependency Inversion Principle promotes the decoupling of high-level modules from low-level modules by introducing abstractions.

The main idea behind DIP is that higher-level modules should not depend on lower-level modules; instead, they should both depend on abstractions.

Benefits of Dependency Inversion Principle

The Dependency Inversion Principle offers several benefits, such as:

  1. Decoupling: By adhering to DIP, the code becomes more modular, making it easier to maintain and extend.
  2. Testability: Decoupled code is easier to test, as each module can be tested independently.
  3. Reusability: Modules become more reusable since they are less dependent on other modules.

Dependency Inversion Principle in C# – The Basics

To implement the Dependency Inversion Principle in C#, you should focus on creating abstractions (interfaces or abstract classes) and making both high-level and low-level modules depend on them. By doing so, you can avoid direct dependencies between high-level and low-level modules.

Dependency Inversion Principle C# Example

To better understand the Dependency Inversion Principle in C#, let’s walk through an example.

Without Dependency Inversion Principle

Imagine you have an email notification system that sends notifications to users. The EmailNotification class has a Send method which directly depends on the SmtpClient class to send the email.

public class EmailNotification
{ private SmtpClient _smtpClient; public EmailNotification() { _smtpClient = new SmtpClient(); } public void Send(string emailAddress, string message) { _smtpClient.Send(emailAddress, message); }
}

This design has a problem: the EmailNotification class is tightly coupled to the SmtpClient class. If you decide to change the email sending mechanism, you’ll need to modify the EmailNotification class.

Implementing Dependency Inversion Principle

To implement the Dependency Inversion Principle, you’ll introduce an abstraction that both the EmailNotification class and the SmtpClient class will depend on. This abstraction will be an interface called IEmailSender.

public interface IEmailSender
{ void Send(string emailAddress, string message);
}

Refactoring the Example with Dependency Inversion Principle

Now, let’s refactor the EmailNotification and SmtpClient classes to depend on the IEmailSender interface. First, make the SmtpClient class implement the IEmailSender interface.

public class SmtpClient : IEmailSender
{ public void Send(string emailAddress, string message) { // Implementation of email sending using SMTP }
}

Next, modify the EmailNotification class to depend on the IEmailSender interface instead of the SmtpClient class. You’ll do this by passing an instance of IEmailSender to the constructor of the EmailNotification class.

public class EmailNotification
{ private IEmailSender _emailSender; public EmailNotification(IEmailSender emailSender) { _emailSender = emailSender; } public void Send(string emailAddress, string message) { _emailSender.Send(emailAddress, message); }
}

Now, the EmailNotification class is no longer directly dependent on the SmtpClient class. If you need to change the email sending mechanism, you can create a new class implementing the IEmailSender interface without modifying the EmailNotification class.

Dependency Inversion Principle and Interfaces

Role of Interfaces in Dependency Inversion Principle

Interfaces play a crucial role in implementing the Dependency Inversion Principle. They act as the abstractions that both high-level and low-level modules depend on, allowing for easy decoupling and flexibility.

Implementing Dependency Inversion Principle with Interfaces in C

In C#, you can utilize interfaces to implement the Dependency Inversion Principle effectively. By defining interfaces for your abstractions, you can create loosely-coupled code that is easier to maintain, test, and extend.

Dependency Inversion Principle and Dependency Injection

Understanding Dependency Injection

Dependency Injection (DI) is a technique used to decouple classes by providing their dependencies from external sources. It is often used in conjunction with the Dependency Inversion Principle to achieve even better decoupling and testability.

Dependency Injection Techniques in C

There are three common techniques for implementing Dependency Injection in C#:

  1. Constructor Injection: Injecting dependencies through the constructor of a class.
  2. Property Injection: Injecting dependencies through public properties of a class.
  3. Method Injection: Injecting dependencies as parameters of a method.

Combining Dependency Inversion Principle and Dependency Injection

By combining the Dependency Inversion Principle and Dependency Injection, you can create highly decoupled and testable code. The Dependency Inversion Principle ensures that modules depend on abstractions, while Dependency Injection provides a way to supply these abstractions to the modules at runtime.

Conclusion

The Dependency Inversion Principle is a powerful concept in object-oriented programming that can greatly improve the quality of your C# code. By following this principle, you can create more maintainable, testable, and reusable code. By combining DIP with Dependency Injection, you can further enhance the flexibility and testability of your applications.

在一个我们比以往任何时候都更加紧密联系的世界中，成为社区的一员可能是释放新机会和实现个人成长的关键。

社区将拥有共同兴趣、热情和目标的志同道合的人聚集在一起，创造归属感并提供协作和学习的平台。

让我们深入了解 5 个社区，无论您是想扩大自己的专业网络、探索新的爱好，还是只是与分享您价值观的其他人建立联系，加入这些社区都会带来长期利益。

总有一个社区适合您。

社区成长的力量

要在社区中真正茁壮成长，重要的是要以超越自身利益的方式做出贡献和回馈。 高价值社区通常会吸引愿意投入时间、精力和资源来创造集体影响力的人，让每个人都受益。

当您成为社区的一员时，重要的不仅仅是您可以从中得到什么，而是您可以给予什么。 通过采取行动并做比自己更重要的事情，您可以产生有意义的影响并激励他人也这样做。 因此，不要只局限于您的直接网络 - 跳跃并探索为更大的社区做出贡献的新方法。

简而言之，成为社区的一员可以成为个人成长和发展的强大催化剂。

领英

从加入群组和参与讨论到分享发人深省的内容和参加活动，有无数种方法可以挖掘 LinkedIn 社区的集体智慧和经验。

此外，LinkedIn 独特的算法可确保您的内容和参与度显示给正确的受众，让您能够建立有意义的关系并确立自己在您所在领域的思想领袖地位。

因此，如果您还没有加入 LinkedIn 社区，现在是时候加入了——成长和影响的机会是无穷无尽的。

  • 这些平台使各级个人能够在全球范围内协作、参与并发表意见。

    也就是说，作为开发人员，我强烈建议使用 Dev 作为您的主要平台。 其用户友好的界面和支持社区使其成为新作家开始建立品牌和扩大影响力的理想平台。

    不要让您的声音被忽视——立即成为这些充满活力的社区的一部分，并开始产生影响。

    • 但 GitHub 不仅适用于开发人员——它还是设计师、作家和其他创意专业人士的中心，他们希望建立自己的作品集并与潜在客户和合作者建立联系。

      超过 290 万个组织和企业，包括 NASA、IBM 和谷歌，依赖 GitHub 进行构建和创新。 此外，超过 72% 的财富 50 强公司使用 GitHub Enterprise 来加速软件开发和改善协作。

      立即加入 GitHub 上的协作社区，释放您的潜力。

      • 产品搜寻

        无论您是有抱负的企业家、经验丰富的产品经理，还是只是喜欢发现最新技术趋势的人，Product Hunt 都能满足每个人的需求。

        通过在 Product Hunt 上列出您的产品，您可以接触到一个充满热情的人网络，他们渴望帮助您成长和成功。 您收到的反馈和见解可能是无价的，支持社区将为您提供所需的推动力，使您的产品更上一层楼。

        • 橱窗

          您可以在 Showwcase 上展示您的热情和工作，从博客和 YouTube 到副业和指导，向更广泛的受众展示，创造更多曝光率和增加潜在机会。

          此外，Showwcase 提供了创建专业发展组合的功能，可以方便地将您的所有职业、激情、工作和展示集中在一个地方，为您节省宝贵的时间和精力。

              这是结识与您有共同爱好或职业目标的志趣相投的人并与他们进行更深层次联系的好方法。 无论您是新来的城市还是只是想拓展业务，Meetup 都可以帮助您找到自己的部落并建立有意义的关系。

              你还在等什么？ 无论是加入当地俱乐部、参加行业活动，还是参与在线社区，都可以通过各种方式参与并开始从社区生活中获益。

              谁知道？ 你可能会发现新的激情，结交终生的朋友，并实现超越你最疯狂梦想的个人成长。 因此，迈出第一步，探索成为社区一员所带来的无限可能性。 世界在等着你。

原文:

In a world where we are more connected than ever before, being a part of a community can be the key to unlocking new opportunities and achieving personal growth.

Communities bring together like-minded individuals who share common interests, passions, and goals, creating a sense of belonging and providing a platform for collaboration and learning.

Let's dive into 5 communities that being a part of will give long-run benefits, whether you're looking to expand your professional network, explore a new hobby, or simply connect with others who share your values.

There's always a community out there for you.

The Power of Community for Growth

To truly thrive within a community, it's important to contribute and give back in ways that go beyond your self-interest. High-value communities often attract people who are willing to invest their time, energy, and resources to create a collective impact that benefits everyone involved.

When you're part of a community, it's not just about what you can get from it - it's about what you can give. By taking action and doing things that are bigger than yourself, you can make a meaningful impact and inspire others to do the same. So don't just limit yourself to your immediate network - leap and explore new ways to contribute to the larger community.

In short, being a part of a community can be a powerful catalyst for personal growth and development.

LinkedIn

LinkedIn is more than just a professional networking site - it's a community-driven platform that brings together individuals from all corners of the globe.
With over 700 million members, LinkedIn provides a space for people to connect, share ideas, and collaborate in ways that can lead to powerful professional and personal growth.

From joining groups and participating in discussions to sharing thought-provoking content and attending events, there are endless ways to tap into the collective wisdom and experience of the LinkedIn community.

Moreover, LinkedIn's unique algorithm ensures that your content and engagement are shown to the right audience, allowing you to build meaningful relationships and establish yourself as a thought leader in your field.

So if you haven't already joined the LinkedIn community, now is the time to do so - the opportunities for growth and impact are endless.

  • www.linkedin.com/in/Anmol-Baranwal/ - Connect with me

DEV / Hashnode / Medium

Dev, Hashnode, and Medium are not just typical blogging platforms, they are thriving communities that offer a plethora of resources for developers and writers to learn, grow, and connect with others who share their passion for technology and creativity. These platforms empower individuals of all levels to collaborate, engage, and make their voices heard on a global scale.

While Dev and Hashnode cater specifically to the developer community, Medium is a more general platform that welcomes writers from all backgrounds and industries. Each platform has its unique features and benefits, from Dev's built-in code editor and easy integration with GitHub to Hashnode's emphasis on community-led learning and Medium's sleek, user-friendly interface.

That being said, as a developer, I highly recommend using Dev as your primary platform. Dev's unique algorithm gives equal visibility and weightage to all writers, regardless of their follower count, ensuring that every voice has a chance to be heard and recognized. Its user-friendly interface and supportive community make it an ideal platform for new writers to start building their brand and expanding their reach.

Don't let your voice go unheard - become a part of these dynamic communities today and start making an impact.

  • dev.to/anmolbaranwal - Follow me here

GitHub

GitHub is more than just a digital storage locker for code - it's a bustling hub of innovation and teamwork where tech enthusiasts from all corners of the globe unite to create, learn, and build together.

With over 70 million repositories and 56 million developers on the platform, GitHub offers a unique opportunity to connect with like-minded individuals, collaborate on projects, and showcase your skills to a wider audience.

But GitHub is not just for developers - it's also a hub for designers, writers, and other creative professionals looking to build their portfolios and connect with potential clients and collaborators.

Over 2.9 million organizations and businesses, including NASA, IBM, and Google, rely on GitHub to build and innovate. Additionally, more than 72% of Fortune 50 companies use GitHub Enterprise to accelerate software development and improve collaboration.

Join the collaborative community on GitHub and unleash your potential today.

  • github.com/Anmol-Baranwal - Follow me Here

Product Hunt

Product Hunt is a bustling community of tech enthusiasts, where makers, investors, and entrepreneurs alike converge to discover the latest and greatest products in the market while fostering a spirit of creativity and community.

With over 5 million monthly active users, Product Hunt provides a unique opportunity to connect with like-minded individuals, share your ideas and products, and get feedback from a global audience.

Whether you're an aspiring entrepreneur, a seasoned product manager, or simply someone who loves discovering the latest tech trends, Product Hunt has something for everyone.

By listing your product on Product Hunt, you can tap into a network of passionate individuals who are eager to help you grow and succeed. The feedback and insights you receive can be invaluable, and the supportive community will give you the boost you need to take your product to the next level.

  • www.producthunt.com/@anmol-baranwal - My Profile

Showwcase

Showwcase is a tech community platform that connects like-minded individuals, from coders and developers to engineers, makers, hackers, data scientists, and designers. Unlike other networks, Showwcase was designed to provide a fresh, beautifully crafted platform where the community is built from the bottom up.

Showwcase allows you to build meaningful connections, network to showcase your work, collaborate, share knowledge, and grow in a safe and secure environment. You can showcase your passion and work on Showwcase, from blogging and YouTube to side hustles and mentoring, to a wider audience, creating more exposure and increasing potential opportunities.

Showwcase is a great way to build meaningful connections and networking, opening doors to discussing ideas, building communities, creating products, providing services, generating revenues, and fueling your passion.

In addition, Showwcase offers a feature to create a professional development portfolio, which conveniently consolidates all of your career, passion, work, and shows in one place, saving you valuable time and effort.

  • www.showwcase.com/anmol-baranwal - New Here :)

Daily

Daily is the ultimate hub for developers who want to stay current with tech news and trends, connect with peers, and showcase their work to a global audience. Reading articles is a great way to boost productivity and acquire new skills by staying updated on the latest industry trends and learning from experts in our field.

Daily takes this one step further by promoting active engagement with shared content. By sharing and commenting on other users' posts, we expand our knowledge and contribute to the community's growth. Daily's unique feature of disallowing self-promotion ensures that the focus is on quality content and genuine interaction.

In summary, reading articles on Daily can increase productivity and foster a sense of community and collaboration.

  • app.daily.dev/Anmol-Baranwal - My Profile

Meetup

Beyond being a social networking platform, Meetup is a community made up of individuals who come together to learn, grow, and share their experiences. With Meetup, you can discover local events and connect with people who share your passions and interests. From hiking groups to book clubs to tech meetups, there's something for everyone on Meetup.

Meetup is especially useful for those who are looking to expand their social circle or explore new interests. It's a great way to meet like-minded people who share your hobbies or professional goals and to connect with them on a deeper level. Whether you're new to a city or simply looking to branch out, Meetup can help you find your tribe and build meaningful relationships.

So what are you waiting for? Whether it's joining a local club, attending industry events, or even engaging with online communities, there's no shortage of ways to get involved and start reaping the benefits of community life.

Who knows? You might just discover new passions, make lifelong friends, and achieve personal growth beyond your wildest dreams. So take the first step and explore the endless possibilities that come with being a part of a community. The world is waiting for you.

If you enjoy my content, show your support by following me on GitHub - Keep building and creating!
